title: Proclamation No. 261, s. 1938
text:
PROCLAMATION NO. 261

ESTABLISHING AS MOUNT DAJO NATIONAL PARK FOR THE BENEFIT AND ENJOYMENT OF THE PEOPLE OF THE PHILIPPINES THE PARCEL OF LAND SITUATED IN THE MUNICIPAL DISTRICTS OF PATIKUL AND TALIPAU, ISLAND OF JOLO, PROVINCE OF SULU.

Upon the recommendation of the Secretary of Agriculture and Commerce, and pursuant to the provisions of section one of Act Numbered Thirty-nine hundred and fifteen, entitled “An Act providing for the establishment of national parks, declaring such parks as game refuges and for other purposes,” I, Manuel L. Quezon, President of the Philippines, hereby reserve for park purposes for the benefit and enjoyment of the people of the Philippines and withdraw from sale, settlement, or other dispositions, subject to private rights, if any there be, the following parcel of the public domain, situated in the municipal districts of Patikul and Talipau, Island of Jolo, Province of Sulu, and described in the Bureau of Forestry map No. N. P.-20 to wit:

Beginning at point marked 1, on Bureau of Forestry map No. N. P-20, a B. L. concrete monument, 15 by 60, identical to corner 6 of H. A. 144689 of Pascual Pantoja; thence N. 24° 51’ W., 144.84 meters to corner 2, a B. L. concrete monument, 15 by 60, identical to corner 5 of H. A. 144689 of Pascual Pantoja; thence S. 84° 39’ W., 360.28 meters to corner 3, a B. L. concrete monument, 15 by 60, identical to corner 4 of H. A. 144689 of Pascual Pantoja; thence S. 18° 58’ W., 277.18 meters to corner 4, a B. L. concrete monument, 15 by 60, identical to corner 3 of H. A. 144689 of Pascual Pantoja; thence N. 88° 02’ W., 90.43 meters to corner 5, a B. L. concrete monument, 15 by 60, identical to corner 17 of lot No. 980, Mount Dajo cadastre case No. 1; thence N. 58° 20’ W., 384.79 meters to corner 6, a B. L. concrete monument, 15 by 60, identical to corner 18 of lot No. 980, Mount Dajo cadastre case No. 1; thence N. 88° 43’ W., 254.99, meters to corner 7, a B. L. concrete monument, 15 by 60, identical to corner 1, of lot No. 980, Mount Dajo cadastre case No. 1; thence N. 56° 54’ W., 426.89 meters to corner 8, a B. L. concrete monument, 15 by 60, identical to corner 2, of lot No. 980, Mount Dajo cadastre case No.1; thence N. 51° 05’ E., 337.74 meters to corner 9, a B. L. concrete monument, 15 by 60, identical to corner 3, of lot No. 980, Mount Dajo cadastre case No. 1, thence N. 8° 38’ E., 837.68 meters to corner 10, a B. L. concrete conument, 15 by 60, identical to corner 4, of lot No. 980, Mount Dajo cadastre case No. 1, thence N. 0° 56’ w., 142.69 meters to corner 11, a B. L. concrete monument, 15 by 60, identical to corner 5, of lot No. 980, Mount Dajo cadastre case No. 1; thence S. 82° 14’ E., 168.58 meters to corner 12, a B. L. concrete monument, 15 by 60, identical to corner 6, of lot No. 980, Mount Dajo cadastre case No. 1; thence N. 34° 51’ E., 149.34 meters to corner 13, a B. L. concrete monument, 15 by 60, identical to corner 7, of lot No. 980, Mount Dajo cadastre case No. 1; thence S. 26° 01’ E., 128.09 meters to corner 14, a B. L. concrete monument, 15 by 60, identical to corner 8, of lot No. 980, Mount Dajo cadastre case No. 1; thence N. 76° 14’ E., 301.83 meters to corner 15, a B. L. concrete monument, 15 by 60, identical to corner 9, of lot No. 980, Mount Dajo cadastre case No. 1; thence S. 77° 16’ E., 585.65 meters to corner 16, a B. L. concrete monument, 15 by 60, identical to corner 10, of lot No. 980, Mount Dajo cadastre case No. 1; thence S. 13° 20’ E., 560.81 meters to corner 17, a B. L. concrete monument, 15 by 60, identical to corner 11, of lot No. 980, Mount Dajo cadastre case No. 1; thence S. 42° 33’ E., 433.25 meters to corner 18, a B. L. concrete monument, 15 by 60, identical to corner 12, of lot No. 980, Mount Dajo cadastre case No. 1; thence S. 44° 45’ E., 109.54 meters to corner 19, a B. L. concrete monument, 15 by 60, identical to corner 13, of lot No. 980, Mount Dajo cadastre case No. 1; thence S. 48° 40’ E., 569.43 meters to corner 20, a B. L. concrete monument, 15 by 60, identical to corner 14, of lot No. 980, Mount Dajo cadastre case No. 1; and thence S. 51° 50’ W., 115.50, meters to corner 1, the point of beginning.

Corner 1 of this national park us S. 88° 55’ W. 983.52 meters, more or less, from B. L. L. M. No. 15, Mount Dajo cadastre No. 99.

Containing an area of approximately 213.3510 hectares.

The said parcel of land shall be known as the “Mount Dajo National Park” and shall remain under the administration of the Bureau of Forestry, subject to the general executive control and supervision of the Secretary of Agriculture and Commerce.

In witness whereof, I have hereunto set my hand and caused the seal of the Commonwealth of the Philippines to be affixed.

Done at the City of Manila, this twenty-eighth day of February, in the year of Our Lord, nineteen hundred and thirty-eight, and of the Commonwealth of the Philippines, the third.

{SEAL}

President of the Philippines

By the President:

Secretary to the President
